com.pax.poslink

Class CommSetting

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.pax.poslink.CommSetting

  • public class CommSetting
extends java.lang.Object
    Set the communication method with POS terminal

    The CommSetting must be set before you call ProcessTrans Methods for the first time.
    If you Don't set the CommSetting properties, program will use the parameter in the file named "commsetting.ini" which is under the specify directory indicate in the parameter of CommSetting construction method.

        construction method
        Parameters:

    • Method Detail

      • setType

        public boolean setType(java.lang.String type)
        Set type

        type can be "UART","TCP", "BLUETOOTH", "SSL", "HTTP", "HTTPS", "USB", "AIDL"

      • getType

        public java.lang.String getType()
        Get the communication type

      • setTimeOut

        public boolean setTimeOut(java.lang.String timeout)
        Set communication timeout.

        -1: no timeout, but -1 only valid for "UART" and "TCP"
        >0 wait n millisecond to timeout.
        measured in 1ms

      • getTimeOut

        public java.lang.String getTimeOut()
        Get value of timeout

      • setSerialPort

        public boolean setSerialPort(java.lang.String serialport)
        Set serial port

        value is "COMx" x is the number 1-6
        If in the Linux environment,value should be "/dev/ttyx",x is the number
        valid only while CommType is UART

      • getSerialPort

        public java.lang.String getSerialPort()
        Get the serial port

      • setBaudRate

        public boolean setBaudRate(java.lang.String baudrate)
        Set the baud rate for serial port

        baudrate can be "1200", "2400", "4800", "9600", "14400", "19600", "38400", "115200"
        valid only while CommType is "UART"

      • getBaudRate

        public java.lang.String getBaudRate()
        Get the value of baud rate

      • setDestIP

        public boolean setDestIP(java.lang.String destip)
        Set the terminal IP

        For example "192.168.1.1"
        valid only while CommType is TCP, SSL, HTTP, HTTPS

      • getDestIP

        public java.lang.String getDestIP()
        Get the value of terminal IP

      • setDestPort

        public boolean setDestPort(java.lang.String destport)
        Set the terminal port

        value is "1" ~"65535"
        valid only while CommType is TCP, SSL, HTTP, HTTPS

      • getDestPort

        public java.lang.String getDestPort()
        Get the value of terminal port

      • setMacAddr

        public boolean setMacAddr(java.lang.String MacAddr)
        Set the MAC of BLUETOOTH communication type

        For example is "00:40:6E:8A"
        valid only while CommType is "BLUETOOTH"

      • getMacAddr

        public java.lang.String getMacAddr()
        Get the value of MAC address

      • getDeviceName

        public java.lang.String getDeviceName()
        Get the vales of deviceName
        Returns:
        deviceName

      • setDeviceName

        public void setDeviceName(java.lang.String deviceName)
        Set the deviceName of USB communication type

        For example is "/dev/pax/usb/001/007"
        valid only while CommType is "USB"
        Corresponding list of PAX device USB ProductName See PAX USB ProductName

        Parameters:
        deviceName - the name of the device. In the standard implementation, this is the path of the device file

      • setEnableProxy

        public void setEnableProxy(boolean enableProxy)

        Proxy is a feature used on POS machine without connection ability, such as Q20 on E500.
        The value is true by default on machine like E500 but false for other devices.

        Ignore this if you are not using that kind of machine. This setting will take effect after you do anything with PosLink.ProcessTrans()
        Parameters:
        enableProxy - whether enable proxy

      • isEnableProxy

        public boolean isEnableProxy()
        Returns:
        whether enable proxy

      • SaveCommSettingFile

        @Deprecated
public int SaveCommSettingFile()
        Deprecated. 
        save the commsetting.ini file. This won't take effect any more.
